steps.title: Apply the circumference formula
steps.content: The circumference of a circle is $C=\pi d$, where $d$ is the diameter. Substitute the given $C=12\pi$: $$12\pi=\pi d.$$

steps.title: Solve for the diameter
steps.content: Divide both sides by $\pi$: $$d=12.$$ The answer is 12.

steps.title: Confirm the answer
steps.content: As a final check, make sure the computed value answers exactly what the question asks for. The work above gives $12$; substituting or interpreting it in the original condition satisfies the requirement, so $12$ is the value to enter.
